sacks_per_game_z.limits: Pass-rushing LBs naturally get more. Use lb_type filter to compare like-for-like.

qb_hits_per_game_z.what: QB hits per game — pressures beyond sacks
qb_hits_per_game_z.limits: Off-ball LBs rarely blitz enough to register many hits.

pressure_rate_z.what: Total pressures (sacks + QB hits) per game
pressure_rate_z.how: (Sacks + QB hits) / games. Also used to classify pass-rusher vs off-ball (threshold: 0.4/game).
pressure_rate_z.limits: Scheme-dependent. Some LBs blitz by design, others never do.

tfl_per_game_z.limits: Both pass-rushing and off-ball LBs can get TFLs. Good for both types.

solo_tackle_rate_z.what: Solo tackles as % of total tackles
solo_tackle_rate_z.limits: Off-ball LBs in tackle-heavy schemes may have lower solo rates due to gang tackling.

tackles_per_snap_z.what: Tackles per defensive snap — involvement rate
tackles_per_snap_z.limits: High rates can mean the defense is giving up yards. Not always positive.

forced_fumbles_per_game_z.limits: Low frequency. Strip-sack specialists inflate this.

passes_defended_per_game_z.what: Passes defended per game — coverage ability
passes_defended_per_game_z.limits: Key differentiator for coverage LBs. Pass rushers rarely register these.

interceptions_per_game_z.limits: Rare event. One lucky play dominates career rates. But elite coverage LBs (like Roquan Smith) consistently get these.
